entries.name: Scienceworks
entries.desc: A science museum operated by Museums Victoria, housed in a former sewage pumping station, with hands-on galleries covering physics, the human body, and sport, plus lightning shows in its dedicated theatre.

photo.alt: Scienceworks museum entrance, Melbourne
photo.credit: <!-- photo: scienceworks-museum-melbourne.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Scienceworks Museum.jpg" by Wai Hong · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Scienceworks_Museum.jpg -->

entries.name: zoom Kindermuseum
entries.desc: Vienna's dedicated children's museum in the MuseumsQuartier complex, presenting large-scale hands-on themed exhibitions that change each season, with separate programmes designed for different age groups from infants to twelve-year-olds.

photo.alt: zoom Kindermuseum entrance in the Vienna MuseumsQuartier courtyard
photo.credit: <!-- photo: zoom-kindermuseum-vienna.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Zoom Kindermuseum MQ Wien.jpg" by Max Wittmann · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Zoom_Kindermuseum_MQ_Wien.jpg -->

entries.name: Technopolis
entries.desc: Belgium's national science centre in Mechelen, with hundreds of hands-on experiments spread across a large dedicated building covering physics, technology, biology, and everyday science for children and families.

photo.alt: Technopolis entrance with its pink cone sculpture, Mechelen
photo.credit: <!-- photo: technopolis-mechelen.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Technopolis-ingang-roze-kegel.jpg" by Technopolis-AF · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Technopolis-ingang-roze-kegel.jpg -->

entries.name: Catavento Cultural
entries.desc: A science and culture museum in São Paulo's historic Palácio das Indústrias, with four large themed pavilions — Universe, Life, Engineering, and Society — holding interactive exhibits aimed at school-age children.

photo.alt: Palácio das Indústrias, the historic building housing Catavento Cultural, São Paulo
photo.credit: <!-- photo: palacio-das-industrias-sao-paulo.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Museu Catavento-Espaço Cultural da Ciência 04.jpg" by Lunagaoka · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Museu_Catavento-Espa%C3%A7o_Cultural_da_Ci%C3%AAncia_04.jpg -->

entries.name: Manitoba Children's Museum
entries.desc: A children's museum set in a restored historic railway building at The Forks, offering hands-on play galleries and a full-size vintage locomotive aimed at younger children and families.

photo.alt: Manitoba Children's Museum entrance at The Forks, Winnipeg
photo.credit: <!-- photo: manitoba-childrens-museum-the-forks-winnipeg.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Entrance to Manitoba Children's Museum at the Forks in Winnipeg, Manitoba.JPG" by Ccyyrree · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Entrance_to_Manitoba_Children%27s_Museum_at_the_Forks_in_Winnipeg,_Manitoba.JPG -->

entries.name: TELUS Spark Science Centre
entries.desc: Calgary's science centre, with interactive galleries on the human body, energy, and creativity, a digital dome theatre, and an outdoor playground known as the Brainasium.

photo.alt: TELUS Spark Science Centre illuminated exterior at night, Calgary
photo.credit: <!-- photo: telus-spark-calgary.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Calgary Sparks Center.JPG" by CharlesAPhillips63 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Calgary_Sparks_Center.JPG -->

entries.name: Experimentarium
entries.desc: A large interactive science centre north of central Copenhagen, filling several floors with hands-on experiments in physics, the human body, and the natural world, linked by a distinctive spiral staircase.

photo.alt: Experimentarium building exterior, Hellerup, Copenhagen
photo.credit: <!-- photo: experimentarium-hellerup-copenhagen.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Experimentarium, Hellerup.jpg" by Andersbruun · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Experimentarium,_Hellerup.jpg -->

entries.name: Heureka – The Finnish Science Centre
entries.desc: Finland's national science centre just north of Helsinki, with hands-on exhibits across physics, technology, and biology, an outdoor experiment park, and a rotating programme of large-scale travelling exhibitions.

photo.alt: Heureka science centre building, Vantaa, Finland
photo.credit: <!-- photo: heureka-science-centre-vantaa.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Heureka.jpg" by Danila Talikov · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Heureka.jpg -->

entries.name: Cité des Enfants – Cité des Sciences et de l'Industrie
entries.desc: The children's wing of Europe's largest science museum at Parc de la Villette, with separate discovery zones designed for different age groups covering the body, communication, and building.

photo.alt: Cité des Sciences et de l'Industrie building, Parc de la Villette, Paris
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cite-des-sciences-paris.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.5 · "La cite des sciences 2.jpg" by Eric Pouhier · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:La_cite_des_sciences_2.jpg -->

entries.name: Labyrinth Kindermuseum
entries.desc: A children's museum in Berlin built around a single large themed hands-on exhibition that changes over time, encouraging open-ended play and learning for primary-school-age children.

photo.alt: Labyrinth Kindermuseum building facade, Berlin
photo.credit: <!-- photo: labyrinth-kindermuseum-berlin-facade.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Labyrinth-Kindermuseum-Berlin-Gebäudefasade.jpg" by Gesundbrunnen Berlin · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Labyrinth-Kindermuseum-Berlin-Geb%C3%A4udefasade.jpg -->

entries.name: Universum® Bremen
entries.desc: A hands-on science centre in Bremen housed in a distinctive whale-shaped shell structure, covering life, earth, and technology through interactive experiments and demonstrations aimed at families and school groups.

photo.alt: Universum Bremen science centre, whale-shaped building exterior
photo.credit: <!-- photo: universum-bremen.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Universum Bremen087.jpg" by Matthias Süßen · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Universum_Bremen087.jpg -->

entries.name: Nehru Science Centre
entries.desc: A national interactive science centre in Mumbai run under India's science museum council, combining hands-on indoor exhibits with an outdoor science park of large mechanical and physics installations.

photo.alt: Entrance of Nehru Science Centre, Mumbai
photo.credit: <!-- photo: nehru-science-centre-mumbai.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Entrance of Nehru Science Center.JPG" by Suyogaerospace at English Wikipedia · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Entrance_of_Nehru_Science_Center.JPG -->

meta: Largest Interactive Science Centre in India

entries.name: Explora – Il Museo dei Bambini di Roma
entries.desc: Rome's children's museum, built around a kid-scale role-play town where younger children learn through pretend jobs and hands-on activities, with visits typically run in timed sessions.

photo.alt: Explora children's museum building, Rome
photo.credit: <!-- photo: explora-museo-bambini-roma.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Explora il Museo dei bambini di Roma.jpg" by Romaapiedi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Explora_il_Museo_dei_bambini_di_Roma.jpg -->

entries.name: MUBA – Museo dei Bambini Milano
entries.desc: Milan's children's museum, set in the historic Rotonda della Besana, presenting play and art workshops and hands-on exhibitions that children explore in guided timed sessions.

photo.alt: Rotonda della Besana, the historic building housing MUBA, Milan
photo.credit: <!-- photo: rotonda-della-besana-milano.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Milano - Rotonda della Besana 0321.jpg" by Phyrexian · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Milano_-_Rotonda_della_Besana_0321.jpg -->

entries.name: Miraikan – National Museum of Emerging Science and Innovation
entries.desc: Japan's national museum of emerging science on Odaiba, known for its suspended Geo-Cosmos LED globe and exhibits on robotics, space, and cutting-edge research, tied to the ASIMO robot's history.

photo.alt: Miraikan science museum building, Odaiba, Tokyo
photo.credit: <!-- photo: miraikan-tokyo.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public domain · "Miraikan.jpg" by Jason Ruck · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Miraikan.jpg -->

entries.name: NEMO Science Museum
entries.desc: A science museum in a green copper building shaped like a ship's hull, designed by Renzo Piano, with five floors of hands-on exhibits on science and technology and a rooftop terrace over the harbour.

photo.alt: NEMO's green copper ship-hull-shaped building rising over Amsterdam's Oosterdok harbour
photo.credit: <!-- photo: nemo-science-museum-amsterdam.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Nemo science museum Amsterdam.jpg" by Klaas `Z4us` van B. V · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Nemo_science_museum_Amsterdam.jpg -->

entries.name: Science Centre Singapore
entries.desc: Singapore's national science centre, holding over a thousand interactive exhibits across multiple galleries, along with an outdoor water-play area and adjacent attractions such as its Snow City and dome theatre.

photo.alt: Entrance of Science Centre Singapore with its twin spire towers in Jurong East
photo.credit: <!-- photo: science-centre-singapore.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Science Centre Singapore - 20151121.jpg" by Smuconlaw · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Science_Centre_Singapore_-_20151121.jpg -->

entries.name: CosmoCaixa Barcelona
entries.desc: Barcelona's science museum, run by the "la Caixa" foundation, best known for a large indoor recreation of a flooded Amazon rainforest alongside hands-on halls on physics, geology, and evolution.

photo.alt: CosmoCaixa's converted 1904 modernist brick building with the "la Caixa" star logo, Barcelona
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cosmocaixa-barcelona.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "CosmoCaixa Museu Barcelona.JPG" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:CosmoCaixa_Museu_Barcelona.JPG -->

entries.name: Junibacken
entries.desc: A children's cultural attraction on Stockholm's Djurgården island themed around Astrid Lindgren's stories, centred on the Story Train ride through fairytale scenes plus a Pippi Longstocking play house.

photo.alt: Junibacken's waterfront building on Djurgården island with the Nordic Museum behind it, Stockholm
photo.credit: <!-- photo: junibacken-stockholm.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Junibacken.jpg" by Esquilo · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Junibacken.jpg -->

entries.name: Tom Tits Experiment
entries.desc: A large science centre southwest of Stockholm with hundreds of hands-on experiments spread across several floors and a surrounding outdoor park of water, physics, and optical illusion installations.

photo.alt: Tom Tits Experiment's red-brick former industrial building in Södertälje
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tom-tits-experiment-sodertalje.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Storgatan 33 Svenska Centrifug Tom Tits Experiment Södertälje 2005.jpg" by Fredrika Mellander Rönn · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Storgatan_33_Svenska_Centrifug_Tom_Tits_Experiment_S%C3%B6dert%C3%A4lje_2005.jpg -->

entries.name: Swiss Science Center Technorama
entries.desc: A Swiss science centre near Zurich focused on hands-on physics and natural phenomena, with hundreds of experiment stations and live demonstrations of high-voltage electricity and other effects.

photo.alt: Exterior of the Swiss Science Center Technorama building with its rooftop signage, Winterthur
photo.credit: <!-- photo: technorama-winterthur.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Swiss Science Center Technorama - panoramio.jpg" by qwesy qwesy · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Swiss_Science_Center_Technorama_-_panoramio.jpg -->

entries.name: Eureka! The National Children's Museum
entries.desc: The UK's national children's museum, purpose-built for younger children with interactive galleries on the body, everyday life, and the wider world, letting children role-play in a mini town and shop.

photo.alt: Eureka! The National Children's Museum exterior, Halifax
photo.credit: <!-- photo: eureka-national-childrens-museum-halifax.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Exterior of Eureka! The National Children's Museum.jpg" by Sospot · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Exterior_of_Eureka!_The_National_Children%27s_Museum.jpg -->

entries.name: We the Curious
entries.desc: Bristol's hands-on science centre on the harbourside, with interactive exhibits spread across multiple floors on the body, biology, and technology, alongside a full-dome planetarium and a programme of live science shows.

photo.alt: The Planetarium building of We the Curious science centre, Millennium Square, Bristol
photo.credit: <!-- photo: we-the-curious-planetarium-bristol.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "The Planetarium, Millennium Square, Bristol - geograph.org.uk - 2234710.jpg" by Anthony O'Neil · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Planetarium,_Millennium_Square,_Bristol_-_geograph.org.uk_-_2234710.jpg -->

entries.name: Natural History Museum
entries.desc: One of the world's great natural history institutions in South Kensington, free to enter, anchored by a 25-metre blue whale skeleton in its central hall and galleries on dinosaurs, volcanoes, and human evolution that draw families from around the world.

photo.alt: Natural History Museum, London
photo.credit: <!-- photo: natural-history-museum-london.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "The Natural History Museum, London - DSCF0401" by Rept0n1x · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Natural_History_Museum,_London_-_DSCF0401.JPG -->

entries.name: The Children's Museum of Indianapolis
entries.desc: The largest children's museum in the world, spanning several floors with exhibits on dinosaurs, science, and world cultures, including the Dinosphere paleontology hall and outdoor sports experience.

photo.alt: Dinosaur sculptures bursting through the Dinosphere dome at The Children's Museum of Indianapolis
photo.credit: <!-- photo: childrens-museum-of-indianapolis-dinosphere.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Brontosaurus at TCMI by Volkan Yuksel 7-11-09 DSC02702 PN 24387230 cs.jpg" by Volkan Yuksel · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Brontosaurus_at_TCMI_by_Volkan_Yuksel_7-11-09_DSC02702_PN_24387230_cs.jpg -->

meta: Largest Children's Museum in the World

entries.name: Brooklyn Children's Museum
entries.desc: Founded as the world's first museum designed specifically for children, this Crown Heights institution offers hands-on cultural, natural-history, and world-culture exhibits for young visitors.

photo.alt: Brooklyn Children's Museum exterior, Crown Heights, New York City
photo.credit: <!-- photo: brooklyn-childrens-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Brooklyn Children's Museum (52142426214).jpg" by ajay_suresh · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Brooklyn_Children%27s_Museum_(52142426214).jpg -->

entries.name: Boston Children's Museum
entries.desc: One of the oldest children's museums in the United States, on Boston's waterfront and marked by its giant milk-bottle landmark, with hands-on exhibits on construction, science, and everyday life.

photo.alt: Boston Children's Museum with the giant Hood Milk Bottle landmark, Fort Point waterfront
photo.credit: <!-- photo: boston-childrens-museum-hood-milk-bottle.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Boston, Museum Wharf, Hood Milk Bottle.jpg" by openroads.com · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Boston,_Museum_Wharf,_Hood_Milk_Bottle.jpg -->

meta: Among the Oldest Children's Museums in the US

entries.name: Please Touch Museum
entries.desc: Philadelphia's children's museum, housed in the grand Memorial Hall from the 1876 Centennial Exhibition, centred on hands-on play galleries and a restored early-twentieth-century carousel.

photo.alt: Memorial Hall in Fairmount Park, Philadelphia, home of the Please Touch Museum
photo.credit: <!-- photo: memorial-hall-philadelphia.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Memorial Hall in Fairmount Park, Philadelphia, PA.JPG" by Ngilmour3 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Memorial_Hall_in_Fairmount_Park,_Philadelphia,_PA.JPG -->

entries.name: The Strong National Museum of Play
entries.desc: A museum devoted entirely to the history and culture of play, home to the National Toy Hall of Fame and the World Video Game Hall of Fame, with interactive exhibits on toys, games, and childhood.

photo.alt: Exterior of The Strong National Museum of Play in Rochester, New York
photo.credit: <!-- photo: strong-museum-of-play-rochester.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "External view of Museum of Play.jpg" by Misc. · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:External_view_of_Museum_of_Play.jpg -->

meta: Home of the National Toy Hall of Fame

entries.name: Children's Museum of Houston
entries.desc: A children's museum in Houston's Museum District known for its bilingual exhibits, with hands-on galleries on science, building, and community roles designed for younger children.

photo.alt: Neoclassical facade of the Children's Museum of Houston in the Museum District
photo.credit: <!-- photo: childrens-museum-houston-facade.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "HoustonChildrenMuseum.JPG" by WhisperToMe · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:HoustonChildrenMuseum.JPG -->

entries.name: Children's Museum of Pittsburgh
entries.desc: A children's museum on Pittsburgh's North Side blending historic and modern buildings, known for its MAKESHOP maker space, water-play hall, and rotating hands-on art and science exhibits.

photo.alt: Exterior of the Children's Museum of Pittsburgh on the North Side
photo.credit: <!-- photo: childrens-museum-pittsburgh-exterior.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Children's Museum of Pittsburgh.jpg" by Popscreenshot · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Children%27s_Museum_of_Pittsburgh.jpg -->

entries.name: Minnesota Children's Museum
entries.desc: A children's museum in downtown St. Paul with several themed galleries built around active play, including a multi-story climbing structure and hands-on science and creativity spaces.

photo.alt: Downtown St. Paul, Minnesota skyline
photo.credit: <!-- photo: st-paul-skyline-minnesota.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Saint Paul, Minnesota 9.jpg" by John Polo · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Saint_Paul,_Minnesota_9.jpg -->

entries.name: Port Discovery Children's Museum
entries.desc: Baltimore's children's museum near the Inner Harbor, built around interactive play galleries and a tall multi-story climbing structure aimed at younger children and families.

photo.alt: Baltimore skyline seen from the Inner Harbor
photo.credit: <!-- photo: baltimore-inner-harbor.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Bmoreharbor.JPG" by Oldlinestate · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bmoreharbor.JPG -->

entries.name: Bay Area Discovery Museum
entries.desc: A children's museum just north of San Francisco at Fort Baker, set beneath the Golden Gate Bridge, with indoor discovery galleries and an outdoor campus for nature-based exploration.

photo.alt: Golden Gate Bridge seen from a vista point in Sausalito, near Fort Baker
photo.credit: <!-- photo: golden-gate-bridge-sausalito.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Sausalito (CA, USA), Golden Gate Bridge Vista Point -- 2022 -- 3066.jpg" by Dietmar Rabich · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sausalito_(CA,_USA),_Golden_Gate_Bridge_Vista_Point_--_2022_--_3066.jpg -->

entries.name: New York Hall of Science
entries.desc: A science museum in Corona, Queens built for the 1964 World's Fair, with hands-on exhibits on biology, chemistry, and technology, plus one of the largest outdoor science playgrounds in the United States.

photo.alt: Exterior of the New York Hall of Science in Flushing Meadows-Corona Park, Queens
photo.credit: <!-- photo: new-york-hall-of-science.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "New York Hall of Science (exterior)" by Edgar Alfonseca · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:New_York_Hall_of_Science_(exterior).jpg -->

entries.name: Chicago Children's Museum
entries.desc: Chicago's children's museum on Navy Pier, with hands-on play galleries covering construction, water, art, and prehistory, anchored by the Tinkering Lab maker space and a three-story climbing structure.

photo.alt: Navy Pier on the Chicago lakefront
photo.credit: <!-- photo: navy-pier-chicago.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 1.0 · "Navy Pier" by J. Miers · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Navy_Pier.JPG -->

entries.name: COSI
entries.desc: Columbus's science and technology museum on the Scioto riverfront, with large hands-on galleries on space, energy, gadgets, and the ocean, plus a giant-screen theatre and an outdoor science park along the river.

photo.alt: COSI seen across the Scioto River in Columbus, Ohio
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cosi-columbus-scioto-river.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Center of Science and Industry, Columbus, Ohio, from across the Scioto River" by Minh Nguyen · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Center_of_Science_and_Industry,_Columbus,_Ohio,_from_across_the_Scioto_River.jpg -->

entries.name: Children's Museum of Manhattan
entries.desc: A children's museum on the Upper West Side with five floors of hands-on exhibits on science, media, literacy, and early childhood, designed for younger children from birth through age ten.

photo.alt: Children's Museum of Manhattan entrance on West 83rd Street
photo.credit: <!-- photo: childrens-museum-of-manhattan.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Children's Museum of Manhattan" by Jim.henderson · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Childrens_Museum_of_Manhattan_212_W83_jeh.jpg -->

entries.name: Adventure Science Center
entries.desc: Nashville's science museum with large interactive galleries on the human body, space, and technology, anchored by the Sudekum Planetarium dome theatre and hands-on exhibits spanning multiple floors.

photo.alt: Nashville skyline viewed from Fort Negley, near the museum's location
photo.credit: <!-- photo: nashville-skyline.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Nashville skyline from Fort Negley 2018" by Jacknstock · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Nashville_skyline_from_Fort_Negley_2018.jpg -->

entries.name: Denver Children's Museum
entries.desc: A children's museum on Denver's Platte River with hands-on indoor galleries and a large outdoor nature-themed playground, built primarily for younger children exploring science, art, and the natural world.

photo.alt: Children's Museum of Denver at Marsico Campus
photo.credit: <!-- photo: childrens-museum-of-denver.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Children's Museum of Denver at Marsico Campus" by ZoeO · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:20150804_childrens-museum_120.jpg -->

entries.name: Perot Museum of Nature and Science
entries.desc: Dallas's natural science museum in a bold geometric building designed by Thom Mayne, with eleven permanent halls covering paleontology, energy, engineering, and space, alongside a dedicated children's discovery area.

photo.alt: Perot Museum of Nature and Science exterior in Dallas
photo.credit: <!-- photo: perot-museum-dallas.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Perot Museum in Dallas, Texas, exterior view from southeast" by ScheWo · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:20230312_ScheWo_Perot_Museum_in_Dallas,_Texas,_exterior_view_from_southeast.jpg -->

entries.name: California Science Center
entries.desc: A free science museum in Exposition Park housing Space Shuttle Endeavour vertically in a dedicated pavilion, alongside interactive galleries on ecosystems, the human body, and air and space, making it one of the most visited in the country.

photo.alt: California Science Center at Exposition Park, Los Angeles
photo.credit: <!-- photo: california-science-center-exposition-park.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "California Science Center at Exposition Park" by atgorden · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:California_Science_Center_at_Exposition_Park_-_panoramio.jpg -->

entries.name: Oregon Museum of Science and Industry (OMSI)
entries.desc: Portland's science museum on the east bank of the Willamette River, with five large exhibit halls on physics, life science, and earth science, a docked USS Blueback submarine open for tours, and a full-dome planetarium.

photo.alt: OMSI's exterior center cube building on the east bank of the Willamette River, Portland, Oregon
photo.credit: <!-- photo: omsi-building-portland.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "OMSI center cube from west - Portland, Oregon.JPG" by M.O. Stevens · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:OMSI_center_cube_from_west_-_Portland,_Oregon.JPG -->

entries.name: Pacific Science Center
entries.desc: A science centre in Seattle Center built for the 1962 World's Fair, with galleries on dinosaurs, mathematics, technology, and the human body, plus a live tropical butterfly house and a laser dome theatre.

photo.alt: The iconic Gothic arches of Pacific Science Center at Seattle Center, built for the 1962 World's Fair
photo.credit: <!-- photo: pacific-science-center-arches-seattle.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Pacific Science Center arches, Seattle WA.jpg" by Ron Clausen · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Pacific_Science_Center_arches,_Seattle_WA.jpg -->

still_needed: Questacon – National Science and Technology Centre
